items.headline: Los Gatos to hold municipal election on November 3, 2026
items.summary: City Council adopted a resolution calling a general municipal election for Tuesday, November 3, 2026, to fill three council seats.

items.headline: Final map approved for Tract 10675 Boulevard development
items.summary: City Council approved the final subdivision map for Tract 10675 Boulevard, clearing the way for the project to proceed to construction.

items.headline: $1.3M commercial project permitted on South 10th Street
items.summary: A commercial/industrial building received a permit at 1935 S 10th St. The project's scale and location in an industrial corridor could affect traffic and land use in that area.

items.headline: Semiconductor equipment installation permitted at North 1st Street
items.summary: Intermolecular received a $600,000 permit for a chlorine trifluoride gas cabinet installation at 3011 N 1st St, indicating continued advanced manufacturing activity in San José's tech corridor.

items.headline: Parks and Recreation Strategic Plan Adopted
items.summary: City Council adopted a new strategic plan for parks and recreation. The plan sets direction for how Mountain View will manage and develop its parks and recreation services.

items.headline: City Decarbonization Five-Year Plan to be Presented
items.summary: Council will hear a study session on Mountain View's decarbonization goal and a proposed five-year plan with specific approach and actions to reduce emissions.

items.headline: City to spend $1.6M on highway patrol traffic management for World Cup
items.summary: Santa Clara City Council and Stadium Authority approved a standard agreement with the California Highway Patrol for traffic management services during FIFA World Cup events, with a projected cost of $1.6 million and related budget amendment.

items.headline: Planning Commission to review East Campbell Avenue line project
items.summary: The Planning Commission will hear a presentation on a draft East Campbell Avenue line project. The meeting will also cover the capital improvement plan for citywide projects to ensure alignment with the Campbell 2040 general plan.
